content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. The only difference is that in the easy version, NNN is guaranteed to be equal to MMM.**

You are given two arrays AAA and BBB, of lengths NNN and MMM respectively, each consisting of positive integers.

Your score is initially 000.

You can perform the following operation any number of times, as long as both arrays AAA and BBB are non-empty:


Select an integer XXX from AAA, and an integer YYY from BBB, such that X‚Č†YX \neq YXÓÄ†=Y.
Remove one occurrence of XXX from AAA, and one occurrence of YYY from BBB.
Add (X+Y)(X + Y)(X+Y) to your score.
Your task is to find the maximum possible score that you can obtain.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚ÄĒ the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the sizes of arrays AAA and BBB, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BMB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_MB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BM‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum score you can get.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: A=[2,2]A = [2, 2]A=[2,2] and B=[2]B = [2]B=[2]. It's not possible to choose two different elements from AAA and BBB at all; so the score is 000.
Test case 222: A=[2,1]A = [2, 1]A=[2,1] and B=[2]B = [2]B=[2]. We can choose 111 from AAA and 222 from BBB, adding 1+2=31+2 = 31+2=3 to our score.
After this, A=[2]A = [2]A=[2] and B=[]B = []B=[] so no further moves can be made.
Test case 333: A=[3,4]A = [3, 4]A=[3,4] and B=[3,3,4,4]B = [3, 3, 4, 4]B=[3,3,4,4]. We can do the following:

Choose 333 from AAA and 444 from BBB. This adds 777 to the score.
After this, A=[4]A = [4]A=[4] and B=[3,3,4]B = [3, 3, 4]B=[3,3,4].
Next, choose 444 from AAA and 333 from BBB. This adds 777 to the score.
After this, A=[]A = []A=[] and B=[3,4]B = [3, 4]B=[3,4].
The score is now 7+7=147+7 = 147+7=14, and no more moves can be made.
It can be proved that a score larger than 141414 cannot be obtained.
